title: Implement conversation-mediated dispatch and consumer groups
purpose: Delivers conversation-mediated dispatch (ADR-007) — the mechanism that replaces Kafka-style consumer group rebalancing with per-message, fault-aware consumer selection. A dispatch conversation is a beamr process that selects a consumer via the group's routing function, links to it, observes completion, and re-routes on crash. Consumer groups are modeled as a routing function over a mutable consumer set; adding or removing a consumer takes effect immediately without pausing other consumers.
task: Create routing/dispatch.rs with the dispatch conversation implementation, and routing/group.rs with the consumer group model. The dispatch conversation receives a message, evaluates the group's routing function to select a consumer, links to the selected consumer process, forwards the message, and observes completion. If the consumer crashes, the link fires immediately (sub-millisecond via beamr process links) and the conversation re-routes to another consumer. Consumer groups are a routing function over a consumer set — a mutable collection of active consumers. Adding or removing a consumer updates the set atomically; the next dispatch conversation sees the change. No stop-the-world rebalance, no partition reassignment. This brief does not implement pressure policies or monitoring — those belong to ROUTING-006.

requirements.title: Implement dispatch conversation consumer selection
requirements.spec: WHEN a message is dispatched to a consumer group, THE SYSTEM SHALL spawn a dispatch conversation process that evaluates the group's routing function to select a target consumer. The routing function SHALL receive the message and the current consumer state view. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT select consumers randomly — selection is always via the routing function.
acceptance: Dispatching a message to a group with three consumers invokes the group's routing function.
The routing function receives the message and a ConsumerStateView for each available consumer.
The selected consumer is the one returned by the routing function.
The dispatch conversation is a beamr process, not a synchronous function call.

requirements.title: Link dispatch conversation to selected consumer via process link
requirements.spec: WHEN a dispatch conversation selects a consumer, THE SYSTEM SHALL establish a beamr process link between the dispatch conversation process and the selected consumer process. The link SHALL provide immediate notification of consumer termination. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use polling or heartbeat-based liveness detection.
acceptance: After consumer selection, a beamr process link exists between the dispatch conversation and the consumer.
The link is established before the message is forwarded to the consumer.
Link establishment does not require consumer cooperation — it is unilateral from the dispatch process.

requirements.title: Implement crash detection and re-routing within one millisecond
requirements.spec: IF the linked consumer process crashes during message processing, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL detect the crash via the process link and re-route the message to another available consumer. Crash detection and re-routing initiation SHALL occur within one millisecond of consumer termination. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT re-route to the crashed consumer.
acceptance: When a consumer crashes mid-processing, the dispatch conversation receives a link-down notification.
The dispatch conversation re-evaluates the routing function excluding the crashed consumer.
Re-routing selects a different consumer from the group.
The elapsed time between consumer crash and re-route initiation is under one millisecond (verified by timestamp comparison in test).
If no other consumers are available, the dispatch conversation returns an error.

requirements.title: Define consumer group as routing function over consumer set
requirements.spec: The routing module SHALL define a ConsumerGroup type that associates a routing function with a mutable set of active consumers. The ConsumerGroup SHALL expose the routing function and the current consumer set to the dispatch conversation. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use partition assignment or static consumer-to-partition mapping.
acceptance: ConsumerGroup::new(routing_function) creates a group with an empty consumer set.
ConsumerGroup exposes its routing function and current consumer set.
The consumer set is an ordered, deduplicated collection of consumer identifiers.
ConsumerGroup implements Debug.

requirements.title: Implement seamless consumer addition to groups
requirements.spec: WHEN a consumer is added to a group, THE SYSTEM SHALL update the consumer set such that the next dispatch conversation sees the new consumer. Adding a consumer SHALL NOT pause, block, or restart dispatch for other consumers in the same group. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT trigger a rebalance protocol.
acceptance: Adding consumer C to a group with consumers [A, B] results in the set [A, B, C].
A dispatch conversation started after the addition can select consumer C.
A dispatch conversation already in progress (started before the addition) completes normally without seeing C.
Adding a consumer during active dispatch does not block or error.

requirements.title: Implement seamless consumer removal from groups
requirements.spec: WHEN a consumer is removed from a group, THE SYSTEM SHALL update the consumer set such that the next dispatch conversation excludes the removed consumer. Removing a consumer SHALL NOT trigger a rebalance or pause other consumers. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT remove a consumer that is processing a message until the dispatch conversation completes or detects failure.
acceptance: Removing consumer B from a group with consumers [A, B, C] results in the set [A, C].
A dispatch conversation started after the removal does not select consumer B.
An in-flight dispatch conversation linked to consumer B continues normally until completion or failure.
Removing a consumer during active dispatch to other consumers does not block or error.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement pressure policy enforcement or monitoring — those belong to ROUTING-006.
SHALL NOT define predicate types or evaluation logic — those belong to ROUTING-001 and ROUTING-002.
SHALL NOT implement routing function loading or compilation — those belong to ROUTING-003.
SHALL NOT implement consumer group persistence — routing state is reconstructed from re-registration per design non-goals.
SHALL NOT implement multi-node dispatch or cross-node consumer groups — single-node only per design non-goals.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal --all-targets -- -D warnings passes clean.
cargo test -p liminal passes with all dispatch and consumer group tests green.
dispatch.rs and group.rs are each under 500 lines including tests (CN1).
Crash re-routing test verifies sub-millisecond detection (CN7).
